columns.title: Be concise and clear
columns.content:
- What is the purpose of the invitation and who is it for
- Avoid lengthy intros, complex jargon, and wordy sentences.

columns.title: Be personal and inviting
columns.content:
- Use a warm, welcoming tone that feels like a chat over coffee​
- Avoid formal expressions, e.g., ”Dear guest," "we hereby," and "You.”​ However, do not become too informal and use expressions like ”Hey there!"

columns.title: Highlight the event's benefit for the recipient
columns.content: - Emphasize what our clients will learn or enjoy during the event

# SEB Talks: Embracing a New Era of Savings​

In times of great uncertainty and rapid change, managing savings has become more challenging than ever. With this in mind, we extend a warm invitation to our digital seminar, where experts will share insights on navigating savings in this new era.​

Among other questions, we will delve into how the stock market changed during the Covid-19 pandemic. We'll also examine the pandemic's lasting effects on the business and innovation climate, and discuss approaches for savers to navigate the current situation.​

We're pleased to have a lineup of respected experts, including savings influencer Börshajen Andra Farhad, SEB's Lina Andersson, an expert in interest rates, Johan Hagbarth, SEB's equity strategist, and Anette Andersson, a sustainability specialist within SEB's fund company. The conversation will be moderated by SEB's personal finance expert Jens Magnusson.​

Warm welcome!

columns.title: 1. Spark interest with the headline
columns.content:
*”SEB Talks: Embracing a New Era of Savings”*

A good headline captures interest even before the email is opened and offers insight into the event's subject.​

More examples of headlines:​  
*"Wrap up the Year with Us at Täcka Udden" ,"Exploring Sweden's Entrepreneurial Environment: SEB Talks"​*

Avoid vague headlines like:​  
*”Welcome to a Seminar at SEB”*

columns.title: 2. Start with the key message
columns.content:
*”In times of great uncertainty and rapid change, managing savings has become more challenging than ever. With this in mind, we extend a warm invitation to our digital seminar, where experts will share insights on navigating savings in this new era.”*

The subject, theme, or reason for the invitation should be evident right from the beginning.​

The reader should be able to quickly assess whether the event is of interest.​

Avoid long and unclear descriptions like:​*”Ever since 1856 – when André Oskar Wallenberg founded the bank – we at SEB…" or "Spring is around the corner and soon nature will awaken again…”*

columns.title: 3. Delve deeper in the second paragraph
columns.content:
*”Among other questions, we will delve into how the stock market changed during the Covid-19 pandemic. We'll also examine the pandemic's lasting effects on the business and innovation climate, and discuss approaches for savers to navigate the current situation.”*

Give more details about the subjects covered or what will happen during the event –like what the attendee will experience.​

For instance, if it's about a dinner  
*"Please join us for a delightful meal, connect with  fellow attendees, and engage in discussions about our vision for 2023.”​*

Avoid internal expressions or complicated terminology.

columns.title: 4. Highlight the participants in the third paragraph
columns.content:
*”We're pleased to have a lineup of respected experts, including savings influencer Börshajen Andra Farhad, SEB's Lina Andersson, an expert in interest rates, Johan Hagbarth, SEB's equity strategist, and Anette Andersson, a sustainability specialist within SEB's fund company. The conversation will be moderated by SEB's personal finance expert Jens Magnusson.”*

Contributing experts often lend weight to the event and enhance interest.​

sections.title: Some tips of phrases to use

columns.title: Avoid
columns.content:
- Dinner with wine​
- Kind regards​
- RSVP​
- First come, first served.​
- Don't forget to notify...

columns.title: Write like this instead
columns.content:
- Dinner​
- Warmly welcome!​
- Registration​
- Limited spots available.​
- Remember to notify...
